task_completion.description: Open the Wazuh local rules file for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, or a GUI editor) is open displaying the contents of '/var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml'.
The XML file contains a rule tag for 5402 with attributes setting it to level 0 and overwrite to yes, e.g., <rule id="5402" level="0" overwrite="yes">.

task_completion.description: Preserve the original logic for rule 5402.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the rule 5402 block, there are tags preserving the logic: <if_sid>5401</if_sid> and <match>; USER=root ; COMMAND=</match>.

task_completion.description: Create a new child rule with ID 100100 for parent rule 5501.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The XML file contains a new rule block starting with <rule id="100100" level="0"> and includes <if_sid>5501</if_sid> to link it to the parent rule.

task_completion.description: Configure the child rule to suppress alerts for the 'backup_svc' user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the rule 100100 block, there is a tag targeting the user, such as <user>backup_svc</user> or <match>backup_svc</match>.

task_completion.description: Apply the changes to the Wazuh manager.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal shows a command being executed to restart the Wazuh manager, such as 'systemctl restart wazuh-manager' or '/var/ossec/bin/wazuh-control restart'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually edited the configuration file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show an actual text editor interface modifying the file, not just echoing text into the void or writing a script without executing it.

integrity.description: Verify the XML modifications are structurally valid.
integrity.visual_evidence: The added rules should be placed correctly within the <group> tags of the XML file, and all opened tags (like <rule>) should be properly closed (</rule>).

integrity.description: Verify the agent genuinely restarted the Wazuh service.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should show the actual execution of the restart command without errors, rather than just typing it without pressing enter.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target file: /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml. Rule 5402 must be overridden (level="0" overwrite="yes") while keeping if_sid: 5401 and match: '; USER=root ; COMMAND='. A new child rule 100100 must be created for parent rule 5501 (if_sid: 5501) at level 0, specifically targeting the user 'backup_svc'.
